reviews.review_text: Planet Coaster is one of those games where you sit down to build a single ride and suddenly lose an entire evening. The coaster creation tools are insanely satisfying, while the management side stays chill enough to never feel stressful. Half the fun is designing a beautiful theme park, and the other half is watching your terrible path layout create complete guest chaos. Creative, relaxing, and dangerously addictive.

It felt incomplete when I picked it up at release so I waited to play a bit more, but it is still incomplete in 2026. Very few themes or blueprints in the base game unlike RTC - I imagine this would be fixed by purchasing the DLC's but that would increase the price point by $100.
The game is fine, but the controls are absolutely terrible, and no easy fixes.

Games decent. Graphic are good. However everything this game does roller coaster tycoon 3 did better, and with mods. Everything from this game is in that game. So why buy a game when they are selling half of it back to you?

Rct3 is on sale right now for less than this. I'd buy that again before this game.

unlimited steam games that steam with no games which is pointless and useless if you steam with no games

Planet Coaster is still one of the best theme park games I've played.

The amount of creativity the game gives you is incredible. You can build a realistic theme park, create something completely crazy, or focus on a specific theme. The choice is entirely yours.

The game also looks great, and even years after release the parks can feel surprisingly realistic. Watching guests walk around, use rides and interact with your park makes everything feel alive.

There is also a nice management side to the game. It's not just about building roller coasters. You have to think about staff, finances, guest happiness, facilities and the overall layout of your park if you want it to succeed.

The DLCs add even more rides, themes and building pieces, giving you even more freedom to create exactly the park you want.

Even in the base game, there are plenty of ways to make your park unique. You can add your own music through speakers, use custom images on posters and screens, and really put your own personal touch on everything you build.

Whether you want to build efficiently, create a realistic park or simply build the most ridiculous roller coaster possible, Planet Coaster gives you all the tools to do it.

Even after all these years, it's still a fantastic game and one I keep coming back to.
Really good game, that is nicely developed and perfect for someone with a bit of creative or engineering flair.

I absolutely love rides and the engineering behind making them so this game is great for me, also it shows the problem areas for rides, shows you how to manage a park, and has kinda accurate roller coaster physics

I really like tycoon games, and Planet Coaster is no exception. Wonderful customization, gameplay, fun scenarios, and a really good sandbox mode paired with good graphics is the definition of a well cared for game.
Sandbox mode is great for a chill experience, and the workshop is great for adding other people's rollercoasters! Would recommend this to anyone.

Having a good time with the game. Used to play Theme Park and Roller Coaster Tycoon, back in the days, but I think this picks it up pretty well. Playing it with my daughter, and we are having a great time making the roller-coasters and making the park look nice. 

While it's not super deep, the depth is still enough to make it interesting. I would recommend it.

I'll just connect this path...oh...nope.. nope I wont. 
I'll just put this small food attraction on this path with an entrance and an exit...Nope...this restaurant has three separate entrances...one is in the back.
It's so fun and relaxing. It's a great way to get creative and also problem solve. The soundtrack is amazing and fgits the game very well. This is one of my favoutite games if not my very favourite.
Genuinely one of my favorite games. I love creating my own theme parks from scratch to figure out what works in the engine. An absolutely goated time killer.

I really wanted to like this game. It just feels like a knock off park management sim. The controls are absolutely atrocious and theres no controller support on PC. Just trying to place the park entrance was enough to dissuade me from playing. 


     The developers expect you to watch someone else play to figure out how to play. Theres no "Tutorial" of any kind, although there are supposedly Hints, which while enabled never appeared for me. The main menu DOES  features a Tutorial Video section, but that was not a pleasant experience. Whenever you start a game mode it just loads in to a game and youre expected to figure it out from that point.

     There are a handful of Game Modes; Career, Sandbox, Challenge, etc. Career mode loads you into a premade park and has a singular pop up that basically says, "Heres a park, make it work". Sandbox mode loads you into a completely blank enviornment with unlimited money and a bunch of convoluted menus. I had to actively hunt just to find a park entrance item menu, which was a nightmare to place proper. Challenge mode i got as far as selecting difficulty and looking at those options i promptly closed the game and uninstalled. 

  As a person who throughly enjoys park sims I was beyond disappointed with this one, Definitely recommend playing any other one on the store

Every time i attempt to enjoy this game, i find myself coming across stupid issues and having to send screenshots through AI to help. stuff like adding a second station to the transport ride and it saying its adjacent to the first, even though its miles away. the game seems illogical. you click on burger hut to manage it, but instead, you are now editing the slice of lettuce on the burger shop roof detail, it is not a good game

I always wanted this Game because I saw youtubers Play it when I was younger.

But Playing it was a big disappointment 😞.

Cons.

I only really have two Bad things to say about this game.

1 It has no tutorial in the game. They did a very Lazy thing and make you go to Youtube with some tutorials (that they made).

2 Almost every cool ride is behind a DLC 
(As far as I can tell) So it's very limiting. 

Pros

1 The Park management was good.
2 The Graphics was Good.
3 The Idea and execution was not bad.

In conclusion if you are ok with going on YouTube to find out how to play the game and you have every DLC then I think it will be very fun.

turned 40 this year... divorce has been rough. house feels completely empty—just me, a folding chair, and the echoing silence. my son barely spoke to me for months. looked right through me. locked in his room, angry at the world, angry at the split. tried everything to reach him. nothing worked. bought planet coaster on a whim because he used to love theme parks when he was little. sat down together. built a completely broken, janky coaster that launched guests straight into a hot dog stall. he laughed. a real, loud, snorting laugh... first time i'd heard it in over a year.

now it's our weekend ritual. we don't talk about the lawyers or the new apartment. we don't have to. we just spend five hours hyper-focusing on the lighting of a single fairytale bathroom or designing absolute death traps... honestly gave me my boy back. can't even express what that means. thank you to the developers... you saved more than just my weekends.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]PC Requirements[/h1]
☐ Run paint
☐ Potato
☐ Rich boi
☑ Ask NASA if they have a spare quantum computer once you place 50,000 individual bushes
☐ Literal house fire

[h1]Gameplay[/h1]
☐ Press W to win
☐ Brainrot
☑ Spending six hours perfectly sculpting a single custom fantasy rock formation
☐ Total anarchy
☐ Building an underground coaster that inflicts 75G of pure force on helpless digital citizens

[h1]Grind[/h1]
☐ Non-existent
☑ Finetuning path connections until your eyes bleed
☐ Standard RPG levels
☐ Finishing your dream park before your real-world children grow up and leave for college
☐ Soul-crushing

[h1]Audio[/h1]
☐ Muted
☐ Generic elevator music
☑ The main menu theme permanently looping in your nightmares
☐ Delightful sounds of guest terror
☐ Pure dopamine

[h1]Bugs[/h1]
☐ None
☐ Unplayable garbage
☑ 400 guests trapped in an infinite walking loop because a queue line was 1 millimeter off
☐ Features, not bugs
☐ Physics engine giving up entirely when a train crashes at Mach 3
